summary: Merged a large matching decompilation of the first item.c assembly slice. The PR added src/melee/it/item.c with roughly 2977 lines replacing most of asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s text, wired item.c into obj_files.mk, expanded item/item-related headers, and adjusted dependent Ness yoyo and grlib callsites to the new item struct/function prototypes. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added src/melee/it/item.c as a new C translation unit for the item code beginning at 0x80266F3C, with decompiled functions such as func_80266F3C, func_80266F70, func_80266FA8, func_80266FCC, func_80267130, item creation/state/think functions, and item drop/throw helpers.

Removed about 4946 lines from asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s, including the old assembly bodies for the newly decompiled item functions and their local constants/strings, while leaving later assembly/data still present in the file.

Added item.c.o to obj_files.mk immediately before asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s.o so the C object takes over the matched symbols while the remaining assembly object continues to provide non-decompiled content.

Expanded src/melee/it/item.h from a minimal ItemData placeholder into a broad item header with Article, ItemAttr, Item, SpawnItem, hitbox/hurtbox, dynamic bone, callback table, and item function declarations.
Renamed the old ItemData concept to Article in item.h and updated Ness yoyo users from xC4_data/x4_ext_attr to xC4_article_data/x4_specialAttributes.
Added dedicated item attribute/variable headers for Ness-related items: itPKFlash.h, itPKThunder.h, and itYoyo.h.
Added include/common_structs.h with shared bitfield/color-overlay structures such as UnkFlagStruct2, ColorOverlay, and r13_ColAnimStruct.

Added item allocator/common-data support structs to src/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h, including ItemCommonData, HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct, and related unknown allocator structs used by item initialization.
Corrected function prototypes in include/functions.h, including func_8000BDB4 returning f32 and func_8001044C taking a second void* plus reordered/scalar argument types.
Adjusted grlib.c to match the corrected func_8001044C prototype by moving the 0.0f argument into the fourth parameter position.

Changed an existing fighter assembly branch target label from it_Throw to func_8026AC74, reflecting that the old named symbol in the item assembly was now represented as an address-style function name and declared in item.h as another item drop function.
smart_moves: Kept the remaining asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s object in the build while adding item.c.o, enabling an incremental asm-to-C replacement instead of requiring the whole assembly file to be eliminated at once.

Reconstructed the core item layout deeply enough for dependent code to use meaningful fields such as xC4_article_data, xCC_item_attr, xD0_itemStateDesc, x5CC_currentAnimFrame, itdmg.xC64_reflectGObj, and xDD4_itemVar.
Used Article as a better name for item article data and propagated that name into Ness yoyo code, reducing misleading ItemData usage.
Created separate headers for PK Flash, PK Thunder, and Yoyo attributes/vars instead of putting all special-item structs directly in item.h.
Used offset-prefixed field names and comments for uncertain data, preserving layout evidence while making the C readable and match-friendly.
Included decomp.me scratch links beside many new item.c functions, leaving a matching audit trail for future maintainers.
decomp_lessons: For large decomp slices, it is practical to replace only the matched text section while retaining the assembly file for undecompiled functions, jump tables, data, and later labels.

When a newly decompiled module exposes real structure layout, dependent modules should be updated immediately; here Ness yoyo code switched from placeholder ItemData fields to Article and specialAttributes fields.
Prototype fixes often cascade outside the target system: correcting func_8001044C required a matching grlib.c callsite update.
Obj allocator globals and r13/common-data globals may need temporary unknown structs to match initialization code before names are fully understood.
Offset-prefixed field names are useful for matching and cross-file migration because they preserve binary layout even when semantic names are incomplete.

Leaving uncertainty in comments, such as 'Init item struct?', 'Initialize item coordinates?', and 'This is actually another Item Drop function', is preferable to overclaiming during early module decompilation.
naming_conventions: Item article data was named Article, with fields x0_common_attr, x4_specialAttributes, xC_itemStates, and x10_modelDesc.
Item struct fields used xOFFSET_semantic_name style, for example xC4_article_data, xCC_item_attr, x518_ownerGObj, x5CC_currentAnimFrame, xD44_lifeTimer, and xD54_throwNum.
Callbacks in item headers used xOFFSET_callback_Event style, for example x4_callback_OnSpawn, x14_callback_OnThrow, xD14_callback_Anim, and xD20_callback_Accessory.
Item-specific attributes used uppercase-ish semantic suffixes, for example x24_YOYO_RELEASE_VEL_X, x0_PKTHUNDER_LIFETIME, and x28_FLASH_EXPLOSION_DELAY.
Unknown or incomplete layout names retained x-offset and Unk markers, such as HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct, UnkItemArticles, x1C_struct, and xDD4_itemVar.
Include guards in added headers used lower/mixed-case names such as _common_structs_h_, _itPKFlash_h_, _itPKThunder_h_, and _itYoyo_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved matched functions out of asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s into src/melee/it/item.c and removed the corresponding assembly bodies.
Inserted src/melee/it/item.c.o into TEXT_O_FILES before the remaining item assembly object to satisfy symbol order and keep unresolved assembly coverage.
Represented constants seen in assembly as C literals where needed, including -1.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, and the double constant used for y-rotation in func_80267130.
Matched JObj mutation code by preserving assert behavior against jobj.h line numbers and calling HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub under the same flag conditions.
Handled bitfields with explicit nested structs/unions for flags such as xDC8_word.flags.x19, xDCC_flag.b0, CameraBoxFlags, and UnkFlagStruct2.
Kept address-style function names for many unknown item functions, while adding comments with tentative behavior rather than premature final names.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice, so no direct reviewer feedback is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8026AC74 or func_8026AD20 to semantic item drop/throw names.
Search for follow-up cleanup of Article, ItemAttr, SpawnItem, and ItemCommonData field names introduced in PR 394.
Look for subsequent PRs that move item allocator structs out of objalloc.h or refine HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct.
Find PRs that complete the remaining asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s assembly after this initial item.c split.
Search for later review or commits that validate the PKFlash, PKThunder, and Yoyo attribute layouts.
